current_activity: Single-task backend discovery lane to capture the MVP backend boundary, candidate API/persistence decision points, and lane-local dependencies from the provided planning inputs.
Fast-path discovery plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Plan the frontend discovery lane as one read-only task that reconciles MVP UI behavior, frontend-owned boundaries, and UI-facing contract questions for the React todo experience.
Fast-path discovery plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the middleware discovery lane as one read-only reconciliation task that captures integration boundaries, unresolved contract questions, dependency edges, and candidate handoffs for later phases without redefining frontend or backend ownership.
Fast-path discovery plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Middleware discovery is reconciled to an integration-only scope: define the frontend-backend todo contract, persistence touchpoints, validation/error mediation, and the minimal delivery/test path, without taking ownership of primary frontend or backend implementation. Discovery also identified later-phase dependency edges from middleware design into frontend consumption, backend API/storage behavior, and end-to-end review expectations; no concrete outbound handoff is required in this lane during discovery.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Backend discovery scope is reconciled to a minimal MVP: one backend-owned service exposing CRUD operations for todo items with durable persistence across reloads, owning the todo data model, validation, storage behavior, and the shared API contract for later phases. Discovery also narrows design-time unknowns to backend-owned choices only: server stack, persistence implementation, endpoint shape, identifier strategy, and minimal error semantics.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Frontend discovery scope was reconciled against the injected objective charter and capability hints. The MVP UI behaviors are limited to viewing a persisted todo list, creating a todo, editing and saving/canceling title changes, toggling complete/incomplete state, and deleting items. Frontend ownership stays within React UI behavior, local interaction state, simple validation, and loading/empty/error presentation; backend API shape, persistence behavior, and shared integration contracts remain upstream-owned. Canonical API and integration contracts were not injected in Resolved Inputs, so the remaining UI-facing contract items are recorded explicitly as carry-forward consumer constraints rather than treated as resolved.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Single-task frontend design reconciliation plan for the React todo MVP, keeping scope limited to UI behavior, consumer-facing integration assumptions, and validation criteria inside the frontend lane.
Fast-path design plan for React web frontend for creating, viewing, completing, editing, and deleting todo items: let the frontend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.
Plan the backend design lane as one producing task that emits the MVP backend design package and the backend-owned todo API contract for downstream consumption.
Fast-path design plan for Simple backend API and persistence layer for storing todo items: let the backend manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Plan the middleware design lane as one read-only reconciliation task that converts discovery findings into a minimal integration design decision set and validation criteria without emitting final lane artifacts or outbound handoffs.
Fast-path design plan for Basic application integration and delivery workflow connecting frontend and backend: let the middleware manager produce the minimal task graph directly.

Reconciled the middleware design lane into one minimal integration decision set: the shared todo contract uses backend-owned string IDs with CRUD operations over a stable JSON resource shape (`id`, `text`, `completed`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`); create/list/read/update/delete must cover edit and complete/uncomplete through partial updates; MVP persistence is a single-node durable backend store that survives browser reloads and backend restarts on the same machine; validation and error mediation use consistent JSON error responses for invalid text, malformed or missing identifiers, not-found items, and unexpected server failures; delivery validation is satisfied by proving end-to-end create, list, edit, toggle, delete, and restart persistence behavior with no remaining intra-lane dependencies or outbound handoffs.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Frontend design scope is reconciled to the chartered MVP: the React UI is bounded to listing persisted todos, creating items, editing titles with save/cancel, toggling complete/incomplete state, deleting items, and presenting simple loading, empty, validation, and error states. Shared API and integration contracts were not injected, so design scope keeps those items as frontend consumer assumptions rather than redefining upstream contracts.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Authored the backend design package for the MVP todo service, selecting Fastify plus SQLite and defining the authoritative CRUD OpenAPI contract with validation, identifier, persistence, and error semantics.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Plan the backend MVP lane as a small serialized build: implement the SQLite persistence module first, wire the Fastify bootstrap/server/routes against the approved CRUD contract next, then add API tests and emit the MVP report, review bundle, and readiness assertion from the immediate validation step.
Plan one backend MVP build lane that turns the approved Fastify plus SQLite design package into a working todo API with persistence, task-local validation, and an acceptance handoff bundle.

Plan the frontend MVP lane as three small worker tasks: establish the React entrypoint and API client against the backend-owned contract, build the todo interaction UI and styling, then add focused frontend tests and emit the required MVP review artifacts for acceptance.
A single frontend MVP build lane should implement the approved React todo experience against the backend-owned contract, then hand a concrete review bundle to acceptance.
Codex turn completed.
